evals.prompt: I just added a messages.send mutation and messages.listByChannel query to convex/messages.ts. Update the swagger docs.
evals.expected_output: openapi.yaml updated with two new path entries for messages:send and messages:listByChannel, using correct Convex request/response conventions

assertions.name: contains_messages_send_path
assertions.description: The spec includes a path entry for messages:send mutation

assertions.name: contains_messages_listByChannel_path
assertions.description: The spec includes a path entry for messages:listByChannel query

assertions.name: send_uses_mutation_endpoint
assertions.description: messages:send is documented under /api/mutation (not /api/query)

assertions.name: listByChannel_uses_query_endpoint
assertions.description: messages:listByChannel is documented under /api/query

assertions.name: send_args_include_channelId_and_body
assertions.description: The send mutation example args include channelId and body

assertions.name: valid_yaml
assertions.description: The output is valid YAML

assertions.name: preserves_existing_endpoints
assertions.description: Existing endpoints (users:getMe, channels:list etc.) are still present

evals.prompt: Our swagger is out of date, can you rescan all the convex functions and rebuild the openapi.yaml?
evals.expected_output: Complete openapi.yaml covering all public functions from users.ts, channels.ts, workspaces.ts, and the HTTP webhook

assertions.name: covers_all_user_functions
assertions.description: Includes users:getMe, users:getByWorkosId, users:createOrUpdate
assertions.check: grep -q 'users:getMe' outputs/openapi.yaml && grep -q 'users:getByWorkosId' outputs/openapi.yaml && grep -q 'users:createOrUpdate' outputs/openapi.yaml

assertions.name: covers_all_channel_functions
assertions.description: Includes channels:list, channels:get, channels:create, channels:join
assertions.check: grep -q 'channels:list' outputs/openapi.yaml && grep -q 'channels:get' outputs/openapi.yaml && grep -q 'channels:create' outputs/openapi.yaml && grep -q 'channels:join' outputs/openapi.yaml

assertions.name: covers_workspace_functions
assertions.description: Includes workspaces:create, workspaces:getBySlug

assertions.name: covers_http_webhook
assertions.description: Includes the /webhooks/workos HTTP endpoint

assertions.name: has_domain_schemas
assertions.description: Defines User, Workspace, Channel schemas in components
assertions.check: grep -q 'User:' outputs/openapi.yaml && grep -q 'Workspace:' outputs/openapi.yaml && grep -q 'Channel:' outputs/openapi.yaml

assertions.name: valid_yaml
assertions.description: The output is valid YAML

assertions.name: no_internal_functions
assertions.description: Does not expose internal/private functions (requireAuth is a helper, not an endpoint)

evals.prompt: Update the swagger docs and publish them to GitHub Pages.
evals.expected_output: Updated openapi.yaml plus a publish-commands.sh with gh CLI commands for GitHub Pages deployment

assertions.name: publish_script_exists
assertions.description: A publish-commands.sh file was created

assertions.name: uses_redocly_or_swagger_build
assertions.description: The publish script builds static HTML from the spec

assertions.name: references_gh_pages_branch
assertions.description: The script targets the gh-pages branch

assertions.name: uses_gh_cli_or_git_push
assertions.description: The script uses gh CLI or git push to deploy

assertions.name: openapi_yaml_exists
assertions.description: An updated openapi.yaml was also produced

assertions.name: valid_yaml
assertions.description: The openapi.yaml is valid YAML
